steps.title: Spot the specific examples
steps.content: The first sentence states that many mushroom species contain phenolic compounds and names two: 5-O-caffeoylquinic acid and kaempferol. The second sentence then reports exactly which mushrooms Ferreira detected each compound in — concrete instances of the general finding.

steps.title: Apply the correct transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence provides specific instances of the general claim in the first.
  • "However,": signals contrast or contradiction, but the detection of specific compounds in specific mushrooms supports the general finding — it doesn't oppose it. ✗
  • "For this reason,": signals a cause-effect link, but the species-level detections aren't caused by the general finding — they illustrate it. ✗
  • "For example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim, so fits — the per-mushroom data exemplifies the broader phenolic compound finding. ✓
  • "Nevertheless,": signals that something holds true despite an obstacle, but no obstacle or objection appears in the first sentence. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
